This is the implementation of Inclusion Polymorphism.

Define the repository interface. This interface abstracts data access and is responsible for retrieving data from the database, either through Open SQL or BAPI calls.

INTERFACE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo
  PUBLIC .
  TYPES: gty_t_company_code_range       TYPE RANGE OF bukrs,
         gty_t_customer_number_range    TYPE RANGE OF kunnr,
         gty_t_credit_ctrl_area_range   TYPE RANGE OF kkber,
         gty_t_credit_acct_range        TYPE RANGE OF knkli,
         gty_t_acct_assignment_grp_cust TYPE RANGE OF ktokd,
         gty_t_change_date_range        TYPE RANGE OF cddatum.

  TYPES: BEGIN OF gty_s_crdt_master,
           syst_id          TYPE syst_sysid,
           company_code     TYPE bukrs,
           customer_number  TYPE kunnr,
           customer_name    TYPE text100,
           credit_ctrl_area TYPE kkber,
           credit_acct      TYPE knkli,
           credit_acct_name TYPE text100,
           risk_categ       TYPE ctlpc_cm,
           term             TYPE dzterm,
           credit_limit     TYPE klimk,
           currency         TYPE waers,
           credit_blocked   TYPE crblb_cm,
           status_cust      TYPE char10,
         END OF gty_s_crdt_master,

         gty_t_crdt_master TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF gty_s_crdt_master WITH EMPTY KEY.

  METHODS find_all IMPORTING it_company_code_range       TYPE gty_t_company_code_range
                             it_customer_number_range    TYPE gty_t_customer_number_range
                             it_acct_assignment_grp_cust TYPE gty_t_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                             it_credit_ctrl_area_range   TYPE gty_t_credit_ctrl_area_range
                             it_credit_acct_range        TYPE gty_t_credit_acct_range
                             iv_credit_blocked           TYPE crblb_cm
                   RETURNING VALUE(result)               TYPE gty_t_crdt_master.

  METHODS find_delta IMPORTING it_company_code_range       TYPE gty_t_company_code_range
                               it_customer_number_range    TYPE gty_t_customer_number_range
                               it_acct_assignment_grp_cust TYPE gty_t_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                               it_credit_ctrl_area_range   TYPE gty_t_credit_ctrl_area_range
                               it_credit_acct_range        TYPE gty_t_credit_acct_range
                               it_change_date_range        TYPE gty_t_change_date_range
                               iv_credit_blocked           TYPE crblb_cm
                     RETURNING VALUE(result)               TYPE gty_t_crdt_master.

  METHODS find_new_cust IMPORTING it_company_code_range       TYPE gty_t_company_code_range
                                  it_customer_number_range    TYPE gty_t_customer_number_range
                                  it_acct_assignment_grp_cust TYPE gty_t_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                        RETURNING VALUE(result)               TYPE gty_t_crdt_master.

ENDINTERFACE.

Next, define two data service interfaces. These interfaces are responsible for handling data transformation and mapping.

INTERFACE zif_zgxxs_parser
  PUBLIC .

  METHODS parse IMPORTING iv_separator TYPE char1
                          iv_delimiter TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                          it_source_data TYPE ANY TABLE
                          iv_include_header TYPE abap_bool DEFAULT abap_true
                RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE stringtab.

  METHODS parse_binary IMPORTING iv_separator TYPE char1
                          iv_delimiter TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                          it_source_data TYPE ANY TABLE
                          iv_include_header TYPE abap_bool DEFAULT abap_true
                RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE xstringtab.

ENDINTERFACE.

zif_zgxxs_parser defines an abstraction for parsing data from internal table format into a text-based file format.

INTERFACE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser
  PUBLIC .

  TYPES: BEGIN OF lty_s_objectid,
           objectid TYPE cdobjectv,
         END OF lty_s_objectid,

         lty_t_objectid TYPE HASHED TABLE OF lty_s_objectid WITH UNIQUE KEY primary_key COMPONENTS objectid.

  METHODS get_updated_customer IMPORTING it_changed_docs TYPE cdredcd_tab
                               RETURNING VALUE(result)   TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_customer_number_range.

  METHODS get_key_tabs IMPORTING it_changed_docs TYPE cdredcd_tab
                       RETURNING VALUE(result)   TYPE REF TO data.

ENDINTERFACE.

zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser This interface abstracts the pure function responsible for mapping data.

Step 2: Write the Class Implementation

We now move on to the class implementation.

CLASS zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo.
  PROTECTED SECTION.

    TYPES: lty_t_udate_range TYPE RANGE OF cddatum,
           lty_t_objectid_range TYPE RANGE OF cdobjectv,
           lty_t_tabname_range TYPE RANGE OF tabname,
           lty_t_fname_range TYPE RANGE OF fieldname.

    TYPES: lty_t_crdt_master TYPE SORTED TABLE OF zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_s_crdt_master WITH NON-UNIQUE KEY PRIMARY_KEY COMPONENTS status_cust.

    CONSTANTS: BEGIN OF lc_tvarv_name,
                objclass TYPE rvari_vnam VALUE 'ZGXXS_INT_CRED_MAST_OBJCLASS',
                tabname TYPE rvari_vnam VALUE 'ZGXXS_INT_CRED_MAST_TABNAME',
                fname TYPE rvari_vnam VALUE 'ZGXXS_INT_CRED_MAST_FNAME',
                tcode TYPE rvari_vnam VALUE 'ZGXXS_INT_CRED_MAST_TCODE',
               END OF lc_tvarv_name.

    DATA go_service TYPE REF TO zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser.

    METHODS get_updated_customers IMPORTING it_udate_range    TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_change_date_range
                                            it_objectid_range TYPE lty_t_objectid_range
                                  RETURNING VALUE(result)     TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_customer_number_range.

    METHODS get_changed_documents IMPORTING it_udate_range TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_change_date_range
                                            it_objectid    TYPE cdobjectv_range_tab
                                  RETURNING VALUE(result)  TYPE cdredcd_tab.

    METHODS get_variant_variables IMPORTING iv_name TYPE rvari_vnam
                                  RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE tvarvc_t.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_CRDT_MASTER_REPO IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD get_changed_documents.
    DATA lt_cdred TYPE cdredcd_tab.
    DATA ls_result LIKE LINE OF result.
    DATA lt_objectid TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser=>lty_t_objectid.


    DATA(lt_objclass_range) = VALUE cdobjectcl_range_tab( FOR ls_obj IN get_variant_variables( lc_tvarv_name-objclass )
                                                          ( sign = ls_obj-sign option = ls_obj-opti
                                                            low = ls_obj-low high = ls_obj-high ) ).


    DATA(lt_tabname_range) = VALUE lty_t_tabname_range( FOR ls_tabname IN get_variant_variables( lc_tvarv_name-tabname )
                                                          ( sign = ls_tabname-sign option = ls_tabname-opti
                                                            low = ls_tabname-low high = ls_tabname-high ) ).


    DATA(lt_fname_range) = VALUE lty_t_fname_range( FOR ls_fname IN get_variant_variables( lc_tvarv_name-fname )
                                                    ( sign = ls_fname-sign option = ls_fname-opti
                                                      low = ls_fname-low high = ls_fname-high ) ).

    DATA(lt_tcode_range) = VALUE cdtcode_range_tab( FOR ls_tcode IN get_variant_variables( lc_tvarv_name-tcode )
                                                    ( sign = ls_tcode-sign option = ls_tcode-opti
                                                      low = ls_tcode-low high = ls_tcode-high ) ).

    ASSIGN it_udate_range[ 1 ] TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_udate>).
    IF <fs_l_udate> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    DATA(lv_date_until) = COND cddatum( WHEN <fs_l_udate>-high IS INITIAL
                                        THEN <fs_l_udate>-low
                                        ELSE <fs_l_udate>-high ).

    CALL FUNCTION 'CHANGEDOCUMENT_READ_ALL'
      EXPORTING
        i_date_of_change           = <fs_l_udate>-low    " From-change date for search
*       i_time_of_change           = '000000'    " From-change time for search
        i_date_until               = lv_date_until    " Change date up to which you want to search
*       i_time_until               = '235959'    " Latest change time in search
        it_objectclass             = lt_objclass_range    " Range Table Type for CDOBJECTCL for Function Module Interface
        it_objectid                = it_objectid
        it_tcode                   = lt_tcode_range    " Range table type for TCODE for function module interface
        it_tablename               = lt_tabname_range   " Range Table Type for CDTABNAME f. Function Module Interface
      IMPORTING
        et_cdred                   = lt_cdred    " Formatted change documents with header data
      EXCEPTIONS
        missing_input_objectclass  = 1
        missing_input_header       = 2
        no_position_found          = 3
        wrong_access_to_archive    = 4
        time_zone_conversion_error = 5
        read_too_many_entries      = 6
        OTHERS                     = 7.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
*     MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
*                WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    result = VALUE #( FOR line IN lt_cdred
                      WHERE ( fname IN lt_fname_range )
                     ( line ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_updated_customers.
  ...
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_variant_variables.

    SELECT *
      FROM tvarvc
      WHERE name eq _name
    INTO TABLE @result.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo~find_all.
    DATA result_tmp TYPE lty_t_crdt_master.

    " include new customer
    IF it_credit_ctrl_area_range IS INITIAL
      AND it_credit_acct_range IS INITIAL.

      SELECT -sysid AS syst_id
             , b~bukrs AS company_code
             , k~kunnr AS customer_number
             , a~name1 &&  && a~name2 AS customer_name
             , k~kkber AS credit_ctrl_area
             , k~knkli AS credit_acct
             , CASE k~knkli
                WHEN ' ' THEN 
                ELSE n~name1 &&  && n~name2
               END AS credit_acct_name
             , k~ctlpc AS risk_categ
             , b~zterm AS term
             , k~klimk AS credit_limit
            " Begin of changes RH0001
             , t~waers AS currency
             " End of changes RH0001
             , k~crblb AS credit_blocked
             , CASE WHEN a~sperr EQ  AND a~loevm EQ 
                    THEN 'ACTIVE'
                    ELSE 'INACTIVE'
               END AS status_cust
      FROM knb1 AS b
        INNER JOIN t001cm AS c ON b~bukrs = c~bukrs
        INNER JOIN kna1 AS a ON b~kunnr = a~kunnr
        INNER JOIN knkk AS k ON a~kunnr = k~kunnr
                            AND c~kkber = k~kkber
        LEFT OUTER JOIN t014 AS t ON k~kkber = t~kkber "#EC CI_BUFFJOIN
        LEFT OUTER JOIN kna1 AS n ON n~kunnr = k~knkli
      WHERE b~bukrs IN _company_code_range
        AND b~kunnr IN _customer_number_range
        AND a~ktokd IN _acct_assignment_grp_cust
        AND k~kkber IN _credit_ctrl_area_range
        AND k~knkli IN _credit_acct_range
      INTO TABLE @result_tmp.

      LOOP AT zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo~find_new_cust(
            it_company_code_range       = it_company_code_range
            it_customer_number_range    = it_customer_number_range
            it_acct_assignment_grp_cust = it_acct_assignment_grp_cust
        ) INTO DATA(ls_new_cust).

        INSERT ls_new_cust INTO TABLE result_tmp.

      ENDLOOP.


    ELSE.

      SELECT -sysid AS syst_id
             , b~bukrs AS company_code
             , k~kunnr AS customer_number
             , a~name1 &&  && a~name2 AS customer_name
             , k~kkber AS credit_ctrl_area
             , k~knkli AS credit_acct
             , CASE k~knkli
                WHEN ' ' THEN 
                ELSE n~name1 &&  && n~name2
               END AS credit_acct_name
             , k~ctlpc AS risk_categ
             , b~zterm AS term
             , k~klimk AS credit_limit
             , t~waers AS currency
             , k~crblb AS credit_blocked
             , CASE WHEN a~sperr EQ  AND a~loevm EQ 
                    THEN 'ACTIVE'
                    ELSE 'INACTIVE'
               END AS status_cust
      FROM knb1 AS b
        INNER JOIN t001cm AS c ON b~bukrs = c~bukrs
        INNER JOIN kna1 AS a ON b~kunnr = a~kunnr
        INNER JOIN knkk AS k ON a~kunnr = k~kunnr
                            AND c~kkber = k~kkber
        LEFT OUTER JOIN t014 AS t ON k~kkber = t~kkber "#EC CI_BUFFJOIN
        LEFT OUTER JOIN kna1 AS n ON n~kunnr = k~knkli
      WHERE b~bukrs IN _company_code_range
        AND b~kunnr IN _customer_number_range
        AND a~ktokd IN _acct_assignment_grp_cust
        AND k~kkber IN _credit_ctrl_area_range
        AND k~knkli IN _credit_acct_range
      INTO TABLE @result_tmp.

    ENDIF.

    result = SWITCH #( iv_active_cust_only
                       WHEN abap_false THEN CORRESPONDING #( result_tmp )
                       ELSE FILTER #( result_tmp USING KEY primary_key
                                      WHERE status_cust = CONV #( 'ACTIVE' ) ) ).


  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo~find_delta.

    DATA lt_result TYPE SORTED TABLE OF zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_s_crdt_master WITH NON-UNIQUE KEY primary_key
                                                                                      COMPONENTS customer_number company_code credit_ctrl_area.
    DATA lt_result_kna1 TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master.
    DATA lt_result_knb1 TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master.
    DATA lt_result_knkk TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master.
    DATA lt_result_merge TYPE SORTED TABLE OF zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_s_crdt_master WITH NON-UNIQUE KEY primary_key
                                                                                      COMPONENTS customer_number company_code credit_ctrl_area.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab> TYPE zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_service=>gty_t_keytab.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab_kna1> TYPE zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_kna1=>gty_t_keytab_kna1.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab_knb1> TYPE zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_knb1=>gty_t_keytab_knb1.
    go_service = NEW zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_service( ).
    " get changed documents
    DATA(lt_changed_docs) = get_changed_documents(
                            it_udate_range = it_change_date_range
                            it_objectid    = VALUE #( FOR line IN it_customer_number_range
                                                      ( sign = line-sign option = line-option low = line-low high = line-high ) )
                        ).
    IF lt_changed_docs IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    " get updated customers
    DATA(lt_updated_customers) = go_service->get_updated_customer( lt_changed_docs ).
    IF lt_updated_customers IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    " extract data
    lt_result = CORRESPONDING #( zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo~find_all(
       EXPORTING
         it_company_code_range       = it_company_code_range
         it_customer_number_range    = lt_updated_customers
         it_acct_assignment_grp_cust = it_acct_assignment_grp_cust
         it_credit_ctrl_area_range   = it_credit_ctrl_area_range
         it_credit_acct_range        = it_credit_acct_range
         iv_active_cust_only         = iv_active_cust_only
     ) ).
    IF lt_result IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    DATA(lo_keytab) = go_service->get_key_tabs( lt_changed_docs ).
    ASSIGN lo_keytab->* TO <lfs_t_keytab>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab> IS ASSIGNED
      AND <lfs_t_keytab> IS NOT INITIAL.

      lt_result_knkk = FILTER #( lt_result IN <lfs_t_keytab> USING KEY primary_key
                          WHERE customer_number = kunnr
                            AND credit_ctrl_area = kkber ).

    ENDIF.

    go_service = NEW zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_kna1( ).
    DATA(lo_keytab_kna1) = go_service->get_key_tabs( lt_changed_docs ).
    ASSIGN lo_keytab_kna1->* TO <lfs_t_keytab_kna1>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab_kna1> IS ASSIGNED
      AND <lfs_t_keytab_kna1> IS NOT INITIAL.

      lt_result_kna1 = FILTER #( lt_result IN <lfs_t_keytab_kna1> USING KEY primary_key
                                 WHERE customer_number = kunnr ).

    ENDIF.

    go_service = NEW zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_knb1( ).
    DATA(lo_keytab_knb1) = go_service->get_key_tabs( lt_changed_docs ).
    ASSIGN lo_keytab_knb1->* TO <lfs_t_keytab_knb1>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab_knb1> IS ASSIGNED
      AND <lfs_t_keytab_knb1> IS NOT INITIAL.

      lt_result_knb1 = FILTER #( lt_result IN <lfs_t_keytab_knb1> USING KEY primary_key
                                 WHERE customer_number = kunnr
                                   AND company_code = bukrs ).

    ENDIF.

    lt_result_merge = CORRESPONDING #( lt_result_kna1 ).

    LOOP AT lt_result_knb1 INTO DATA(ls_result_knb1).
      IF NOT line_exists( lt_result_merge[ KEY primary_key
                                           COMPONENTS customer_number = ls_result_knb1-customer_number
                                                      company_code = ls_result_knb1-company_code
                                                      credit_ctrl_area = ls_result_knb1-credit_ctrl_area ] ).

        INSERT ls_result_knb1 INTO TABLE lt_result_merge.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

    LOOP AT lt_result_knkk INTO DATA(ls_result_knkk).
      IF NOT line_exists( lt_result_merge[ KEY primary_key
                                           COMPONENTS customer_number = ls_result_knkk-customer_number
                                                      company_code = ls_result_knkk-company_code
                                                      credit_ctrl_area = ls_result_knb1-credit_ctrl_area ] ).

        INSERT ls_result_knkk INTO TABLE lt_result_merge.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

    result = CORRESPONDING #( lt_result_merge ).


  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo~find_new_cust.

    SELECT -sysid AS syst_id
           , b~bukrs AS company_code
           , b~kunnr AS customer_number
           , a~name1 &&  && a~name2 AS customer_name
           , b~zterm AS term
           , CASE WHEN a~sperr EQ  AND a~loevm EQ 
                  THEN 'ACTIVE'
                  ELSE 'INACTIVE'
             END AS status_cust
    FROM knb1 AS b
      INNER JOIN t001cm AS c ON b~bukrs = c~bukrs
      INNER JOIN kna1 AS a ON b~kunnr = a~kunnr
    WHERE b~bukrs IN _company_code_range
      AND b~kunnr IN _customer_number_range
      AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT kunnr
                        FROM knkk AS kn  "#EC CI_BUFFSUBQ
                        WHERE kn~kunnr eq b~kunnr
              )
      AND a~ktokd IN _acct_assignment_grp_cust
      INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @result.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

The class zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo implements the interface zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo and is responsible for retrieving data from the database. As it performs data access operations, this class represents the impure function, where side effects are introduced. In addition to data retrieval, this class depends on the interfacezif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser, which serves as the abstraction of the data service. 

Next, we move on to the implementation of the parser class.

CLASS zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES zif_zgxxs_parser .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
    DATA gt_components TYPE cl_abap_structdescr=>component_table.
    METHODS build_header_line IMPORTING iv_separator   TYPE char1
                                        iv_delimiter   TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                        is_source_data TYPE any
                              RETURNING VALUE(result)  TYPE string.

    METHODS build_data_line IMPORTING iv_separator   TYPE char1
                                      iv_delimiter   TYPE char1 OPTIONAL
                                      is_source_data TYPE any
                            RETURNING VALUE(result)  TYPE string.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_DAT_PARSER IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD build_data_line.
    DATA lv_separator TYPE char1.
    DATA lv_delimiter TYPE char1.

    LOOP AT gt_components INTO DATA(ls_component).
      ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_component-name OF STRUCTURE is_source_data TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_data>).
      IF <fs_l_data> IS ASSIGNED.
        result = |{ result }{ lv_separator }{ lv_delimiter }| &&
                 |{ <fs_l_data> }{ lv_delimiter }|.
        lv_separator = iv_separator.
        lv_delimiter = iv_delimiter.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD build_header_line.

    result = REDUCE #( INIT file_data TYPE string
                       sep = ''
                       delimit = ''
                       FOR line IN gt_components
                       NEXT
                       file_data = |{ file_data }{ sep }{ delimit }{ line-name }{ delimit }|
                       sep = iv_separator
                       delimit = iv_delimiter ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_parser~parse.

    IF it_source_data IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    " get the components
    gt_components = CAST cl_abap_structdescr( CAST cl_abap_tabledescr( cl_abap_tabledescr=>describe_by_data( it_source_data )
      )->get_table_line_type( ) )->get_components( ).

    LOOP AT it_source_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_line>).
      IF sy-tabix EQ 1
        AND iv_include_header EQ abap_true.

        INSERT build_header_line(
             iv_separator   = iv_separator
             iv_delimiter   = iv_delimiter
             is_source_data = <fs_l_line> ) INTO TABLE result.

      ENDIF.

      INSERT build_data_line(
           iv_separator   = iv_separator
           iv_delimiter   = iv_delimiter
           is_source_data = <fs_l_line> ) INTO TABLE result.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_parser~parse_binary.

    DATA lv_result TYPE xstring.

    IF it_source_data IS INITIAL.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    " get the components
    gt_components = CAST cl_abap_structdescr( CAST cl_abap_tabledescr( cl_abap_tabledescr=>describe_by_data( it_source_data )
      )->get_table_line_type( ) )->get_components( ).

    DATA(lo_converter) = cl_abap_conv_out_ce=>create( encoding = 'UTF-8' ).

    LOOP AT it_source_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_line>).

      IF sy-tabix EQ 1
        AND iv_include_header EQ abap_true.

        CLEAR lv_result.

        lo_converter->convert(
        EXPORTING
          data                          = build_header_line( iv_separator   = iv_separator
                                                             iv_delimiter   = iv_delimiter
                                                             is_source_data = <fs_l_line> ) && cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline " Field to Be Converted
        IMPORTING
          buffer                        = lv_result    " Converted Data
        ).

        INSERT lv_result INTO TABLE result.

      ENDIF.

      CLEAR lv_result.

      lo_converter->convert(
        EXPORTING
          data                          = build_data_line( iv_separator   = iv_separator
                                                           iv_delimiter   = iv_delimiter
                                                           is_source_data = <fs_l_line> ) && cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline " Field to Be Converted
        IMPORTING
          buffer                        = lv_result    " Converted Data
      ).
*        CATCH cx_sy_codepage_converter_init.    "
*        CATCH cx_sy_conversion_codepage.    "
*        CATCH cx_parameter_invalid_type.    "
      INSERT lv_result INTO TABLE result.

    ENDLOOP.


  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser_cred_mast DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  INHERITING FROM zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
    METHODS build_header_line REDEFINITION.
    METHODS build_data_line REDEFINITION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS get_currency  IMPORTING is_source_data TYPE any
                          RETURNING VALUE(result)  TYPE waers.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_DAT_PARSER_CRED_MAST IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD build_data_line.
    DATA lv_separator TYPE char1.
    DATA lv_delimiter TYPE char1.
    DATA lv_output TYPE c LENGTH 20.
    DATA lv_source_val TYPE string. " RH0001

    DATA(lv_waers) = get_currency( is_source_data ).

    LOOP AT gt_components INTO DATA(ls_component).
      ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_component-name OF STRUCTURE is_source_data TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_data>).
      IF <fs_l_data> IS ASSIGNED.
        DATA(lt_ddic_obj) = ls_component-type->get_ddic_object( ).
        ASSIGN lt_ddic_obj[ 1 ]-dtyp TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_dtyp>).
        ASSIGN lt_ddic_obj[ 1 ]-tabname TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_tabname>).

        " Begin of change RH0001
        IF <fs_l_dtyp> IS ASSIGNED.
          IF <fs_l_dtyp> EQ 'CURR'.
            WRITE <fs_l_data> TO lv_output NO-GROUPING CURRENCY lv_waers.
            CONDENSE lv_output NO-GAPS.
            result = |{ result }{ lv_separator }{ lv_delimiter }| &&
                   |{ lv_output }{ lv_delimiter }|.

            CONTINUE.

          ENDIF.


        ENDIF.

        IF <fs_l_tabname> IS ASSIGNED.
          IF <fs_l_tabname> EQ 'KUNNR'
            OR <fs_l_tabname> EQ 'KNKLI'.
            lv_source_val = <fs_l_data>.
            DATA(lv_ext_number) = |{ lv_source_val ALPHA = OUT }|.
            CONDENSE lv_ext_number NO-GAPS.
            result = |{ result }{ lv_separator }{ lv_delimiter }| &&
                   |{ lv_ext_number }{ lv_delimiter }|.
            CONTINUE.

          ENDIF.

        ENDIF.

        result = |{ result }{ lv_separator }{ lv_delimiter }| &&
                 |{ <fs_l_data> }{ lv_delimiter }|.
        " End of change RH0001

        lv_separator = iv_separator.
        lv_delimiter = iv_delimiter.

        UNASSIGN: <fs_l_dtyp>, <fs_l_tabname>. " RH0001

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD build_header_line.

    DATA(lt_header_map) = VALUE lty_t_header_map(
      ( name = 'SYST_ID' description = 'SAP System' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'COMPANY_CODE' description = 'Company Code' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CUSTOMER_NUMBER' description = 'Customer Number' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CUSTOMER_NAME' description = 'Customer Name' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CREDIT_CTRL_AREA' description = 'Credit Control Area' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CREDIT_ACCT' description = 'Credit Account' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CREDIT_ACCT_NAME' description = 'Credit Account Name' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'RISK_CATEG' description = 'Risk Category' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'TERM' description = 'Terms of Payment' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CREDIT_LIMIT' description = 'Credit Limit' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CURRENCY' description = 'Currency' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'CREDIT_BLOCKED' description = 'Credit Blocked Remark' ) ##NO_TEXT
      ( name = 'STATUS_CUST' description = 'Status Customer' ) ##NO_TEXT
       ).

    result = REDUCE #( INIT file_data TYPE string
                       sep = ''
                       delimit = ''
                       FOR line IN gt_components
                       LET desc = VALUE string( lt_header_map[ name = line-name ]-description DEFAULT line-name )
                       IN
                       NEXT
                       file_data = |{ file_data }{ sep }{ delimit }{ desc }{ delimit }|
                       sep = iv_separator
                       delimit = iv_delimiter ).


  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_currency.

    LOOP AT gt_components INTO DATA(ls_component).
      ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_component-name OF STRUCTURE is_source_data TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_data>).
      IF <fs_l_data> IS ASSIGNED.
        DATA(lt_ddic_obj) = ls_component-type->get_ddic_object( ).
        ASSIGN lt_ddic_obj[ 1 ]-dtyp TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_l_dtyp>).
        IF <fs_l_dtyp> IS ASSIGNED.
          IF <fs_l_dtyp> EQ 'CUKY'.
            result = <fs_l_data>.

          ENDIF.

        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

There are two implementation classes, organized using a parent–child (inheritance) relationship:

  • zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser
    Acts as the base (parent) class, providing common parsing logic.
  • zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser_cred_mast
    A specialized parser for credit master data, implemented as a child class of the base parser.

To demonstrate that this design is easy to test, we will present a test class for the parser functionality.

*"* use this source file for your ABAP unit test classes
CLASS ltcl_parser DEFINITION FOR TESTING
                  RISK LEVEL HARMLESS
                  DURATION SHORT.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA cut TYPE REF TO zcl_zgxxs_dat_parser_cred_mast.
    METHODS: setup,
             test_initial FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check,
             test_cust_leading_zero FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check,
             test_crcdt_leading_zero FOR TESTING RAISING cx_static_check.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS ltcl_parser IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD setup.
    cut = NEW #( ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD test_initial.

    " given
    DATA(lt_source) = VALUE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master(
                      ( syst_id = 'DUM'
                        company_code = '1687'
                        customer_number = '10199751'
                        customer_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        credit_ctrl_area = 'DUM01'
                        credit_acct = '10199751'
                        credit_acct_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        risk_categ = 'RC1'
                        term = 'TERM01'
                        credit_limit = 34000
                        currency = 'AUD' ) ).

    " when
    DATA(act) = cut->zif_zgxxs_parser~parse(
                iv_separator      = '|'
                it_source_data    = lt_source ).

    " then
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( act = act ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD test_cust_leading_zero.

    " given
    DATA(lt_source) = VALUE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master(
                      ( syst_id = 'DUM'
                        company_code = '1687'
                        customer_number = '0000102723'
                        customer_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        credit_ctrl_area = 'CCT01'
                        credit_acct = '10199751'
                        credit_acct_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        risk_categ = 'RC1'
                        term = 'TERM01'
                        credit_limit = 34000
                        currency = 'AUD' ) ).

    " when
    DATA(act) = cut->zif_zgxxs_parser~parse(
                iv_separator      = '|'
                it_source_data    = lt_source ).

    " then
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals(
      EXPORTING
        act                  = act[ 2 ]    " Data object with current value
        exp                  = `DUM|1687|102723|FREECHOICE BYRON BAY|CCT01|10199751|FREECHOICE BYRON BAY|RC1|TERM01|34000.00|AUD||`
        msg                  = 'Customer Number without leading zeros'    " Description
    ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD test_crcdt_leading_zero.

    " given
    DATA(lt_source) = VALUE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master(
                      ( syst_id = 'DUM'
                        company_code = '1687'
                        customer_number = '0000102723'
                        customer_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        credit_ctrl_area = 'CCT01'
                        credit_acct = '0000100300'
                        credit_acct_name = 'FREECHOICE BYRON BAY'
                        risk_categ = 'RC1'
                        term = 'TERM02'
                        currency = 'AUD' ) ).

    " when
    DATA(act) = cut->zif_zgxxs_parser~parse(
                iv_separator      = '|'
                it_source_data    = lt_source ).

    " then
    cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_equals(
      EXPORTING
        act                  = act[ 2 ]    " Data object with current value
        exp                  = `DUM|1687|102723|FREECHOICE BYRON BAY|CCT01|100300|FREECHOICE BYRON BAY|RC1|TERM02|0.00|AUD||`
        msg                  = 'Customer Number without leading zeros'    " Description
    ).

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Next, we move on to another pure function implementation.

CLASS zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_service DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF gty_s_keytab,
            kunnr TYPE kunnr,
            kkber TYPE kkber,
           END OF gty_s_keytab,

           gty_t_keytab TYPE HASHED TABLE OF gty_s_keytab WITH UNIQUE KEY PRIMARY_KEY COMPONENTS kunnr kkber.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_CRDT_MASTER_SERVICE IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_key_tabs.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab> TYPE gty_t_keytab.

    result = NEW gty_t_keytab( ).
    ASSIGN result->* TO <lfs_t_keytab>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT it_changed_docs INTO DATA(ls_changed_doc)
      WHERE tabname EQ 'KNKK'.

      IF ls_changed_doc-tabkey IS NOT INITIAL.
        DATA(lv_kunnr) = ls_changed_doc-tabkey+3(10).
        DATA(lv_kkber) = ls_changed_doc-tabkey+13(4).
        IF NOT line_exists( <lfs_t_keytab>[ KEY primary_key
                                             COMPONENTS kunnr = lv_kunnr
                                                        kkber = lv_kkber ] ).

          INSERT VALUE gty_s_keytab( kunnr = lv_kunnr
                                     kkber = lv_kkber ) INTO TABLE <lfs_t_keytab>.

        ENDIF.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_updated_customer.
    DATA lt_objectid TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser=>lty_t_objectid.
    DATA ls_result LIKE LINE OF result.

    LOOP AT it_changed_docs INTO DATA(ls_cdred).

      IF ls_cdred-objectid IS NOT INITIAL
        AND NOT line_exists( lt_objectid[ KEY primary_key
                                          COMPONENTS objectid = ls_cdred-objectid ] ).

        ls_result = VALUE #( sign = 'I' option = 'EQ' low = ls_cdred-objectid ).
        INSERT ls_result INTO TABLE result.
        INSERT VALUE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser=>lty_s_objectid( objectid = ls_cdred-objectid ) INTO TABLE lt_objectid.

        CLEAR ls_result.

      ENDIF.


    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_kna1 DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  INHERITING FROM zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_service
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF gty_s_keytab_kna1,
            kunnr TYPE kunnr,
           END OF gty_s_keytab_kna1,

           gty_t_keytab_kna1 TYPE HASHED TABLE OF gty_s_keytab_kna1 WITH UNIQUE KEY PRIMARY_KEY COMPONENTS kunnr.

    METHODS zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_key_tabs REDEFINITION.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_CRDT_MAST_SERV_KNA1 IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_key_tabs.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab> TYPE gty_t_keytab_kna1.

    result = NEW gty_t_keytab_kna1( ).
    ASSIGN result->* TO <lfs_t_keytab>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT it_changed_docs INTO DATA(ls_changed_doc)
      WHERE tabname EQ 'KNA1'.

      IF ls_changed_doc-tabkey IS NOT INITIAL.
        DATA(lv_kunnr) = ls_changed_doc-tabkey+3(10).
        IF NOT line_exists( <lfs_t_keytab>[ KEY primary_key
                                             COMPONENTS kunnr = lv_kunnr ] ).

          INSERT VALUE gty_s_keytab_kna1( kunnr = lv_kunnr ) INTO TABLE <lfs_t_keytab>.

        ENDIF.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_zgxxs_crdt_mast_serv_knb1 DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  INHERITING FROM zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_service
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF gty_s_keytab_knb1,
            kunnr TYPE kunnr,
            bukrs TYPE bukrs,
           END OF gty_s_keytab_knb1,

           gty_t_keytab_knb1 TYPE HASHED TABLE OF gty_s_keytab_knb1 WITH UNIQUE KEY PRIMARY_KEY COMPONENTS kunnr bukrs.

    METHODS zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_key_tabs REDEFINITION.

  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_ZGXXS_CRDT_MAST_SERV_KNB1 IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_data_ser~get_key_tabs.
    FIELD-SYMBOLS <lfs_t_keytab> TYPE gty_t_keytab_knb1.

    result = NEW gty_t_keytab_knb1( ).
    ASSIGN result->* TO <lfs_t_keytab>.
    IF <lfs_t_keytab> IS NOT ASSIGNED.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    LOOP AT it_changed_docs INTO DATA(ls_changed_doc)
      WHERE tabname EQ 'KNB1'.

      IF ls_changed_doc-tabkey IS NOT INITIAL.
        DATA(lv_kunnr) = ls_changed_doc-tabkey+3(10).
        DATA(lv_bukrs) = ls_changed_doc-tabkey+13(4).
        IF NOT line_exists( <lfs_t_keytab>[ KEY primary_key
                                             COMPONENTS kunnr = lv_kunnr
                                                        bukrs = lv_bukrs ] ).

          INSERT VALUE gty_s_keytab_knb1( kunnr = lv_kunnr
                                     bukrs = lv_bukrs ) INTO TABLE <lfs_t_keytab>.

        ENDIF.

      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

We follow the same approach as in the parser implementation by using inheritance.

In this case, we define one base (parent) class and two child classes. The base class contains the common logic, while each child class handles table-specific logic for getting the table keys.

As discussed earlier, the repository class depends on the data service layer, and these implementations are invoked within the repository class. However, with this design, the data service class can be tested independently and deterministically. For repository testing, the data service dependency can be mocked via dependency injection, enabling isolated unit tests without coupling to the actual implementation.

Step 3: Process Orchestration

At this stage, we move on to the main program, where the overall process flow is orchestrated, and the different components are brought together.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  lcl_main=>create_instance( )->run(
    EXPORTING
      iv_run_type   = SWITCH #( p_disp
                                WHEN abap_true THEN lcl_main=>gc_run_type-display
                                ELSE lcl_main=>gc_run_type-interface )
      iv_data_type  = COND #( WHEN rb_delta EQ abap_true
                              THEN lcl_main=>gc_data_type-delta
                              ELSE lcl_main=>gc_data_type-full )
      iv_target_system = SWITCH #( p_local
                                  WHEN abap_true THEN lcl_main=>gc_target_system-local
                                  ELSE lcl_main=>gc_target_system-server )
      it_company_code_range = so_cocd[]
      . . .
  ).
  METHOD run.
    DATA lt_split_credit_master TYPE zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master.
    DATA lt_download_return TYPE bapiret2_t.
    DATA ls_down_ret TYPE bapiret2.

    DATA(lv_tcode) = SWITCH tcode( sy-batch
                                        WHEN abap_true THEN gc_transaction
                                        ELSE sy-tcode ).

    CALL FUNCTION 'AUTHORITY_CHECK_TCODE'
      EXPORTING
        tcode  = lv_tcode    " Transaction Code
      EXCEPTIONS
        ok     = 1
        not_ok = 2
        others = 3.
    IF sy-subrc NE 1.
      MESSAGE |User: { sy-uname } not authorized to execute the tcode| TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.


    DATA(ls_validate_ret) = validate_input(
                            iv_run_type                 = iv_run_type
                            iv_data_type                = iv_data_type
                            iv_target_system            = iv_target_system
                            it_company_code_range       = it_company_code_range
                            it_customer_number_range    = it_customer_number_range
                            it_acct_assignment_grp_cust = it_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                            it_credit_ctrl_area_range   = it_credit_ctrl_area_range
                            it_credit_acct_range        = it_credit_acct_range
                            it_udate_range              = it_udate_range
                            iv_local_file               = iv_local_file
                            iv_server_path              = iv_server_path
                            iv_blocked                  = iv_blocked
                            iv_max_data                 = iv_max_data
                        ).
    IF ls_validate_ret IS NOT INITIAL.
      MESSAGE ls_validate_ret-message TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE ls_validate_ret-type.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    " get data
    go_credit_master_repo = NEW zcl_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo02( ). 
    DATA(lt_credit_master) = SWITCH zif_zgxxs_crdt_master_repo=>gty_t_crdt_master( iv_data_type
                                       WHEN gc_data_type-full THEN go_credit_master_repo->find_all_act(
                                                                       it_company_code_range     = it_company_code_range
                                                                       it_customer_number_range  = it_customer_number_range
                                                                       it_acct_assignment_grp_cust = it_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                                                                       it_credit_ctrl_area_range = it_credit_ctrl_area_range
                                                                       it_credit_acct_range      = it_credit_acct_range
                                                                       iv_active_cust_only       = iv_active_cust_only )
                                       ELSE go_credit_master_repo->find_delta_act(
                                                                       it_company_code_range     = it_company_code_range
                                                                       it_customer_number_range  = it_customer_number_range
                                                                       it_acct_assignment_grp_cust = it_acct_assignment_grp_cust
                                                                       it_credit_ctrl_area_range = it_credit_ctrl_area_range
                                                                       it_credit_acct_range      = it_credit_acct_range
                                                                       it_change_date_range      = it_udate_range
                                                                       iv_active_cust_only       = iv_active_cust_only ) ).

    IF lt_credit_master IS INITIAL.
      MESSAGE TEXT-E01
      TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    IF iv_run_type EQ gc_run_type-interface.

      DATA(lv_counter) = 0.
      LOOP AT lt_credit_master INTO DATA(ls_credit_mast).
        lv_counter = lv_counter + 1.
        INSERT ls_credit_mast INTO TABLE lt_split_credit_master.

        IF lv_counter EQ iv_max_data.
          CLEAR lv_counter.

          INSERT download( it_source_data = lt_split_credit_master
                          iv_target_system = iv_target_system
                          iv_local_file  = iv_local_file
                          iv_server_path = iv_server_path ) INTO TABLE lt_download_return.

          CLEAR lt_split_credit_master.

        ENDIF.

      ENDLOOP.

      IF lt_split_credit_master IS NOT INITIAL.
        INSERT download( it_source_data = lt_split_credit_master
                          iv_target_system = iv_target_system
                          iv_local_file  = iv_local_file
                          iv_server_path = iv_server_path ) INTO TABLE lt_download_return.

      ENDIF.


      IF line_exists( lt_download_return[ type = 'S' ] ).

        WRITE / TEXT-M01
        .

        LOOP AT lt_download_return INTO ls_down_ret.
          WRITE / ls_down_ret-message.

        ENDLOOP.

      ENDIF.

      IF line_exists( lt_download_return[ type = 'E' ] ).

        WRITE / TEXT-M02
        .

        LOOP AT lt_download_return INTO ls_down_ret.
          WRITE / ls_down_ret-message.

        ENDLOOP.

      ENDIF.

    ELSE.
      " display alv
      go_alv = NEW lcl_salv( ).
      go_alv->display_alv( lt_credit_master ).


    ENDIF.


  ENDMETHOD.

Conclusion

This post focuses on the design approach rather than detailed implementation or specific syntax. The goal is to illustrate how functional programming principles can be applied in SAP ABAP at an architectural/ design level.

The sequence of steps presented is not mandatory; it is structured this way to make this blog post easier to follow. In practice, the development approach may vary—developers may choose a top-down or bottom-up approach depending on their preferences.

While every effort has been made to present the concepts clearly, some areas may benefit from further refinement or clarification. Feedback and discussion are always welcome to improve and deepen the understanding of this approach.

References

  1. Serokell — Rust Is Not a Functional Language
    A discussion on what defines functional programming and why certain languages only partially adopt its principles.
    https://serokell.io/blog/rust-is-not-a-functional-language
  2. John Carmack — Carmack on Inlined Code
    Insights on code structure, simplicity, and performance trade-offs in software design.
    http://number-none.com/blow/blog/programming/2014/09/26/carmack-on-inlined-code.html

entry.title: CL_ABAP_PARALLEL: Parallel Processing Without the aRFC Enable Function Modules
entry.content:
The Problem Every ABAP Developer Knows Too Well

You're running a year-end reconciliation report that loops through 80,000 vendor invoices, validates them against GL postings, and stamps each record with an approval status. In a test system with 500 entries it finishes in under a minute. In production it runs for four hours — and the business is calling.

The knee-jerk fix most developers reach for is raw asynchronous RFC (aRFC): sprinkle CALL FUNCTION … STARTING NEW TASK … DESTINATION IN GROUP, wire up callback handlers, manage semaphore flags, and pray that your RFC server group is configured correctly across all application servers. It works — eventually — but the resulting code is fragile, hard to test, and nearly impossible for the next developer to maintain without a war story attached.

There is a better way: CL_ABAP_PARALLEL.

What Is CL_ABAP_PARALLEL?

CL_ABAP_PARALLEL is a standard SAP class (available from SAP Basis 7.54 and fully released in ABAP Cloud) that wraps the complexity of aRFC-based parallel dispatch behind a clean, object-oriented interface. Instead of orchestrating raw function module calls, RFC groups, and callback patterns yourself, you define what each unit of work should do, hand the instances over to the framework, and let it handle the rest — process allocation, dispatching, timeout management, and result collection.

Under the hood, the framework still uses RS_ABAP_PARALLEL and the server dispatch class CL_SSI_DISPATCH, so the performance characteristics are identical to hand-rolled aRFC. The difference is in developer experience and maintainability.

Two Ways to Implement: Choose Your Pattern

SAP provides two distinct implementation strategies. Pick the one that fits your team's style and the complexity of your data contracts.

Pattern 1 — Interface Implementation (IF_ABAP_PARALLEL)

This is the recommended modern approach, particularly for ABAP Cloud and clean-core projects. Your processing class implements the IF_ABAP_PARALLEL interface and carries its own typed input data as instance attributes. No serialization gymnastics required.

"---------------------------------------------------------------------
" Step 1: Define the processing task class
"---------------------------------------------------------------------
CLASS zcl_invoice_validation_task DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_abap_parallel.
    INTERFACES if_serializable_object.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_result,
             invoice_id  TYPE vbeln,
             is_valid    TYPE abap_bool,
             error_msg   TYPE string,
           END OF ty_result.

    METHODS constructor
      IMPORTING
        is_invoice TYPE rbkp.           " MIRO invoice header

    METHODS get_result
      RETURNING VALUE(rs_result) TYPE ty_result.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA ms_invoice TYPE rbkp.
    DATA ms_result  TYPE ty_result.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_invoice_validation_task IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD constructor.
    ms_invoice = is_invoice.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_abap_parallel~do.
    " All validation logic lives here — runs in a separate dialog WP
    ms_result-invoice_id = ms_invoice-belnr.

    " Example: Cross-check the invoice amount against the PO tolerance
    DATA(lv_tolerance) = CONV dec15_5( '0.05' ).   " 5 % tolerance
    DATA lv_po_net TYPE wrbtr.

    SELECT SINGLE netwr INTO lv_po_net
      FROM ekko
      WHERE ebeln = ms_invoice-xblnr.  " vendor doc nr maps to PO

    IF lv_po_net IS NOT INITIAL.
      DATA(lv_deviation) = abs( ms_invoice-rwbtr - lv_po_net ) / lv_po_net.
      IF lv_deviation <= lv_tolerance.
        ms_result-is_valid = abap_true.
      ELSE.
        ms_result-is_valid  = abap_false.
        ms_result-error_msg = |Deviation { lv_deviation * 100 } % exceeds tolerance|.
      ENDIF.
    ELSE.
      ms_result-is_valid  = abap_false.
      ms_result-error_msg = 'No matching purchase order found'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_result.
    rs_result = ms_result.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.


"---------------------------------------------------------------------
" Step 2: Orchestrate the parallel run from your main program / method
"---------------------------------------------------------------------
CLASS zcl_invoice_processor DEFINITION
  PUBLIC FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS run_validation.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_invoice_processor IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD run_validation.

    " Fetch the raw invoice headers to validate
    DATA lt_invoices TYPE TABLE OF rbkp.
    SELECT * FROM rbkp
      INTO TABLE @LT_invoices
      WHERE bukrs = '1000'
        AND bldat >= @( cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) - 30 ).

    " Build one task object per invoice
    DATA lt_tasks TYPE cl_abap_parallel=>t_in_inst_tab.
    LOOP AT lt_invoices ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<invoice>).
      DATA(lo_task) = NEW zcl_invoice_validation_task( <invoice> ).
      APPEND lo_task TO lt_tasks.
    ENDLOOP.

    " Instantiate the framework — use 40 % of available dialog processes
    DATA(lo_parallel) = NEW cl_abap_parallel(
      p_percentage = 40
      p_timeout    = 300   " seconds per task
    ).

    " Fire!
    DATA lt_results TYPE cl_abap_parallel=>t_out_inst_tab.
    lo_parallel->run_inst(
      EXPORTING p_in_tab  = lt_tasks
      IMPORTING p_out_tab = lt_results
    ).

    " Harvest results
    LOOP AT lt_results ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<res>).

      " Check for framework-level errors first (timeouts, system failures)
      IF <res>-message IS NOT INITIAL.
        MESSAGE <res>-message TYPE 'W'.
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF.

      " Cast the generic reference back to our concrete task class
      DATA(lo_finished_task) = CAST zcl_invoice_validation_task( <res>-instance ).
      DATA(ls_val_result)    = lo_finished_task->get_result( ).

      IF ls_val_result-is_valid = abap_false.
        " Write rejection log, trigger workflow, etc.
        cl_demo_output=>write( |Invoice { ls_val_result-invoice_id }: { ls_val_result-error_msg }| ).
      ENDIF.

    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

The key advantage here is type safety. Your invoice data lives in a properly typed class attribute (ms_invoice TYPE rbkp) — no intermediate XSTRING packing or XML transformation. When you cast <res>-instance back to zcl_invoice_validation_task, you get full IntelliSense and compile-time checks in ADT.

Pattern 2 — Inheritance from CL_ABAP_PARALLEL (Classic Style)

In this variant your task class inherits from CL_ABAP_PARALLEL and redefines the DO method. Input and output are exchanged via the inherited P_IN / P_OUT XSTRING parameters, which you serialize and deserialize yourself using EXPORT … TO DATA BUFFER / IMPORT … FROM DATA BUFFER.

This pattern is occasionally useful when working on older Basis releases or when you need to carry a heterogeneous shared payload (P_IN_ALL) across all tasks without repeating it for every record.

CLASS zcl_material_enrichment DEFINITION
  PUBLIC INHERITING FROM cl_abap_parallel
  FINAL CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS do REDEFINITION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_material_enrichment IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD do.
    " Deserialize per-task input
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_input,
             matnr TYPE matnr,
             werks TYPE werks_d,
           END OF ty_input.
    DATA ls_input TYPE ty_input.
    IMPORT task_data = ls_input FROM DATA BUFFER p_in.

    " Do the heavy lifting — e.g. call an external pricing API
    DATA lv_price TYPE p LENGTH 10 DECIMALS 2.
    " ... your logic here ...

    " Serialize the result back
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_output,
             matnr TYPE matnr,
             price TYPE p LENGTH 10 DECIMALS 2,
           END OF ty_output.
    DATA ls_output TYPE ty_output.
    ls_output-matnr = ls_input-matnr.
    ls_output-price = lv_price.
    EXPORT result_data = ls_output TO DATA BUFFER p_out.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Practical tip: Prefer Pattern 1 (interface) for all new development. Reserve Pattern 2 for legacy codebases where you need XSTRING-based data exchange with an existing framework or where shared read-only context (P_IN_ALL) can meaningfully reduce payload size.

Constructor Parameters — What They Mean and How They Interact

One of the most common sources of confusion with CL_ABAP_PARALLEL is the interplay between its four resource-control parameters. Here is a clear breakdown:

Parameter Type Effect
P_PERCENTAGEI (0–100)Use X % of the dialog processes that the system deems available at startup. Takes precedence over P_NUM_TASKS if both are supplied.
P_NUM_TASKSIRequest a fixed number of parallel slots. Ignored if P_PERCENTAGE is also set or if the value exceeds the system maximum.
P_NUM_PROCESSESIHard cap on the total simultaneous processes across all app servers. A safeguard against monopolizing the system.
P_TIMEOUTI (seconds)Maximum wall-clock time a single task may run before the framework marks it as failed and populates <res>-message.
P_LOCAL_SERVERFlagWhen set, all tasks run on the same application server as the calling program. Useful for debugging; avoid in production.

Error Handling You Cannot Ignore

Every entry in p_out_tab (or p_out_inst_tab) has a MESSAGE field. This is not optional to check. If a task times out, encounters a system failure, or the framework cannot dispatch it, the result record will have a non-initial MESSAGE and a blank result. Silently skipping that check means silently dropping data.

LOOP AT lt_results ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<r>).
  IF <r>-message IS NOT INITIAL.
    " Log to application log (SLG1) instead of just writing to screen
    cl_bali_log=>...   " or your preferred logging utility
    CONTINUE.
  ENDIF.
  " ... process normal result ...
ENDLOOP.

Additionally, wrap your entire run_inst call in a TRY … CATCH block for cx_sy_parallel_processing to handle catastrophic dispatch failures gracefully.

Quick Comparison: Before and After

Aspect Raw aRFC Pattern CL_ABAP_PARALLEL
BoilerplateRFC function module + callback handler + semaphore flagsOne class implementing IF_ABAP_PARALLEL
Type safetyEXPORT/IMPORT tables, genericTyped class attributes (Pattern 1)
Error handlingManual SYSTEM-EXCEPTIONS per callBuilt-in MESSAGE field per result
TestabilityDifficult (requires RFC infrastructure)Standard class — full unit-test coverage possible
ABAP Cloud compatibleNo (aRFC not available in Cloud)Yes (CL_ABAP_PARALLEL is released)

Closing Thoughts

CL_ABAP_PARALLEL does not do anything fundamentally new — parallel dialog dispatch has been in ABAP for decades. What it does is remove all the ceremony that made developers avoid it. With the interface-based pattern, adding parallelism to an existing data-processing class is often a matter of implementing one interface, one constructor, and adjusting a handful of lines in the orchestrating method.

If your team has been avoiding parallel processing because the old aRFC boilerplate felt too risky or too expensive to maintain, it is time to revisit that decision. Your business users — and the developers who come after you — will thank you.

entry.title: S/4HANA CDS View Dependency Navigator
entry.content:
If you need S/4HANA data for your analytical project, or you want to understand S/4HANA logic—whatever your S/4HANA information needs might be—you will need CDS views. But how do you find CDS views? You can use the Virtual Data Model and CDS Views page, which provides some information, or simply use my CDS View Dependency Navigator for a more flexible navigation across all CDS views. If this is what you need, then my new blog is for you.

You start your navigation from a selection screen on which you specify a starting CDS view or DDIC table and the navigation direction:

To navigate along the hierarchy and display source code, you click on the desired CDS view/DDIC table in the Name and Source Code columns, respectively. If at any step of your CDS view dependency navigation you take a wrong turn, no problem—just press the F3 Back button to go back. 

As you can see, the Dependency Navigator displays:

  • Dependencies
  • Navigation Path
  • Source Code
  • Important annotations such as Description, Lifecycle Status, Successor, etc.

With the Navigation Path, you will never get lost or lose what you have found. This is how to read the Navigation Path:

  • CDS views up the hierarchy are on your right, and CDS views/DDIC tables down the hierarchy are on your left.
  • The ← and → arrows indicate the navigation direction.

If the end of the CDS view dependency hierarchy is reached, an informational message will be displayed:

If you navigate down the dependency hierarchy and need to see the table structure, it can be done as well:

What else is worth mentioning is that the Navigator’s dependency scope is limited to only the CDS views/DDIC tables used in the selection and only one level up/down, without any associations. This eliminates unnecessary clutter and makes navigation easier. Compare the ADT Dependency Analyzer with the Dependency Navigator for the P_SalesAnalytics CDS view:

I have to acknowledge that the S/4HANA CDS View Dependency Navigator was inspired by the ABAP CDS View Object Flow Program. With the introduction of CDS entities in S/4HANA 2020 (ABAP 7.55), it became obsolete due to missing information about CDS entities. This is because the ABAP CDS View Object Flow Program relied on CDS view DDIC views (the sqlViewName annotation) to navigate along the CDS view hierarchy, which is missing for CDS entities.

I had to rethink the approach to finding dependencies to support both CDS views and CDS entities. The S/4HANA CDS View Dependency Navigator parses CDS view code. All it takes is some knowledge of CDS view syntax and regular expressions (regex). The only complication I encountered when parsing CDS view annotations is the mix of structured and flattened syntax, but this can be addressed by processing all combinations.

Now you know what to do if you need to add more CDS view annotation attributes to the Dependency Navigator. Here is the Dependency Navigator code—enjoy

entry.title: Implementing Chained Actions in Unmanaged SAP RAP
entry.content:
Introduction:

A chain action is when one RAP action calls another action internally using EML: 

Why Chained Actions in Unmanaged RAP?

  • No automatic save handling (you control everything)
  • No framework-managed validations
  • Risk of multiple DB updates / inconsistent state
  • Chaining ensures:
    • Single transactional flow
    • Controlled execution order
    • One final Modify Entities

Architecture Overview: 

UI / EML Call
→ Behaviour Definition (Action Triggered)
→ Behaviour Handler (Main Action Method)
→ Execute Child Action 1 (Validation / Check)
→ Execute Child Action 2 (Preparation / Data Collection)
→ Transactional Buffer (All changes stored in memory)
→ Final MODIFY ENTITIES (Single update)
→ Saver Class (Save Sequence)
→ Database Commit

Implementation: 

  • Step 1: Define Actions In BDEF 
    •  Declare :
      • One Main Action
      • One or More Child Actions

  • Step 2: Implement child Actions in BP class 
METHOD checkBeforeDelivery.

  LOOP AT keys INTO DATA(ls_key).

    READ ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY yi_salesorder
      FIELDS ( Status BillingStatus )
      WITH VALUE #( ( %tky = ls_key-%tky ) )
      RESULT DATA(lt_data).

    READ TABLE lt_data INTO DATA(ls_data) INDEX 1.

    IF ls_data-Status = 'Delivery Created'.

      APPEND VALUE #( %tky = ls_key-%tky ) TO failed-yi_salesorder.

      APPEND VALUE #(
        %tky = ls_key-%tky
        %msg = new_message(
          id       = 'YCHAINED_MESSAGE'
          number   = '001'
          severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
          v1       = 'Delivery already created'
        )
      ) TO reported-yi_salesorder.

    ENDIF.

    IF ls_data-BillingStatus = 'GENERATED'.

      APPEND VALUE #( %tky = ls_key-%tky ) TO failed-yi_salesorder.

      APPEND VALUE #(
        %tky = ls_key-%tky
        %msg = new_message(
          id       = 'YCHAINED_MESSAGE'
          number   = '002'
          severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
          v1       = 'Bill already generated'
        )
      ) TO reported-yi_salesorder.

    ENDIF.

  ENDLOOP.

ENDMETHOD.
METHOD GenerateBill.

  LOOP AT keys INTO DATA(ls_key).

    APPEND VALUE #(
      %tky = ls_key-%tky
      %param-BillingStatus = 'GENERATED'
    ) TO result.

  ENDLOOP.

ENDMETHOD.
  • Step 3: Implement Main Action 
METHOD createdelivery.

  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    EXECUTE checkBeforeDelivery
    FROM CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    FAILED DATA(lt_v_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_v_reported).

  IF lt_v_failed IS NOT INITIAL.
    failed-yi_salesorder   = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( failed-yi_salesorder ) lt_v_failed-yi_salesorder ).
    reported-yi_salesorder = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( reported-yi_salesorder ) lt_v_reported-yi_salesorder ).
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    EXECUTE GenerateBill
    FROM CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_invoice_result_raw)
    FAILED DATA(lt_chain_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_chain_reported).

  failed-yi_salesorder   = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( failed-yi_salesorder ) lt_chain_failed-yi_salesorder ).
  reported-yi_salesorder = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( reported-yi_salesorder ) lt_chain_reported-yi_salesorder ).

  IF lt_chain_failed IS NOT INITIAL.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  DATA(lt_billing_result) = lt_invoice_result_raw.

  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    UPDATE FIELDS ( Status BillingStatus )
    WITH VALUE #( FOR key IN keys (
      %tky = key-%tky
      Status = 'Delivery Created'
      BillingStatus = VALUE #(
        lt_billing_result[ %tky = key-%tky ]-%param-BillingStatus OPTIONAL )
      %control-Status        = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
      %control-BillingStatus = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
    ) )
    FAILED DATA(lt_u_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_u_reported).

  failed-yi_salesorder   = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( failed-yi_salesorder ) lt_u_failed-yi_salesorder ).
  reported-yi_salesorder = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( reported-yi_salesorder ) lt_u_reported-yi_salesorder ).

  IF lt_u_failed IS INITIAL.

    READ ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY yi_salesorder
      ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(lt_result).

    result = VALUE #( FOR ls_res IN lt_result (
      %tky   = ls_res-%tky
      %param = ls_res
    ) ).

  ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.
  • Step 3.1: Execute First Action 
  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    EXECUTE checkBeforeDelivery
    FROM CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    FAILED DATA(lt_v_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_v_reported).
  • Step 3.2: Stop on Error
  IF lt_v_failed IS NOT INITIAL.
    failed-yi_salesorder   = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( failed-yi_salesorder ) lt_v_failed-yi_salesorder ).
    reported-yi_salesorder = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( reported-yi_salesorder ) lt_v_reported-yi_salesorder ).
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.
  • Step 3.3: Execute Second Action
  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    EXECUTE GenerateBill
    FROM CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_invoice_result_raw)
    FAILED DATA(lt_chain_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_chain_reported).
  • Step 3.4: Final Update
  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    UPDATE FIELDS ( Status BillingStatus )
    WITH VALUE #( FOR key IN keys (
      %tky = key-%tky
      Status = 'Delivery Created'
      BillingStatus = VALUE #(
        lt_billing_result[ %tky = key-%tky ]-%param-BillingStatus OPTIONAL )
      %control-Status        = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
      %control-BillingStatus = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
    ) )
    FAILED DATA(lt_u_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_u_reported).
  • Step 3.5: Return Result to UI 
    READ ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY yi_salesorder
      ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(lt_result).

    result = VALUE #( FOR ls_res IN lt_result (
      %tky   = ls_res-%tky
      %param = ls_res
    ) ).
  • Final Flow 

Main Action(create Delivery) 
  ↓ 
EXECUTE action1 (Validation-checkBeforeDelivery) 
  ↓ 
EXECUTE action2 (GenerateBill) 
  ↓ 
Final MODIFY (Update Fields) 
  ↓ 
SAVE → DB 

  • Output:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Conclusion

Chain actions in RAP provide a clean and structured way to execute multiple steps within a single transactional flow. By orchestrating actions through EML, you can break complex logic into smaller, manageable units while still maintaining consistency. 

The architecture ensures that all steps run inside the same transactional buffer, and only the final state persists through a single update and save sequence. This avoids common issues like buffer overwrites and inconsistent data.

entry.title: Pessimistic Concurrency Control (Locking) using Unmanaged Scenario in RAP
entry.content:
Introduction: 

SAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), locking has a major impact because if multiple users can update the same record simultaneously, it leads to data inconsistencies or overwrites. To work on this concurrency control, we have two different approaches-  

  1. Pessimistic concurrency control (locking). 
  2. Optimistic concurrency control (Etag or Odata Etag). 

In a RAP unmanaged scenario, pessimistic concurrency control (locking) is particularly useful because it assumes conflicts are likely and prevents them upfront by locking the record as soon as a user starts editing (like delete, update or modify operations) it. In an unmanaged scenario, the RAP framework does not automatically handle locking. Developers are responsible for implementing the entire locking mechanism manually. 

Pessimistic Concurrency Control (Locking): In RAP business objects, enqueue locks are used for pessimistic concurrency control. Exclusive locking happens on data. 

  • Exclusive Locking: Before an operation (like an UPDATE or DELETE) is executed, the RAP framework acquires an exclusive lock on the specific data instance in a global lock table. 
  • Every time a lock is requested, the system checks the lock table. If another user already holds a lock on that specific record, the new request is rejected immediately by throwing an error message. 
  •  The lock is released in the scenarios like, 
  1. ABAP session gets terminated. 
  2.  LUW gets terminated by calling commit or Rollback. 
  3.  Draft scenario is discarded or expired.

Scenario: 

In this application, we have a Sales (Header) table (ZDB_SALES_HDR) and Sales (Item) table (ZDB_SALES_ITEM) linked through so_id. Need to set the actual database lock by explicitly calling the standard Enqueue Object inside the FOR LOCK method. Users should also be able to save incomplete data as draft and complete it later. To handle custom logic and manual persistence, an unmanaged RAP approach is used. 

1. Database tables:

@EndUserText.label : 'Header sales table' 
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 
define table zdb_sales_hdr { 

key client  : abap.clnt not null; 
key so_id   : abap.char(10) not null; 
customer_id : abap.char(10); 
order_date  : sydatum; 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'zdb_sales_hdr.currency' 
total_amount: abap.curr(10,2); 
currency    : abap.cuky; 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode : ' zdb_sales_hdr.currency' 
discount_pct: abap.curr(10,2); 
status      : zde_or_status; 
created_by  : syuname; 
created_on  : timestampl; 
approved_by : syuname; 
approved_on : sydatum; 
}

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Item sales table' 
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE 
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT 
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A 
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED 

define table zdb_sales_item { 
key client  : abap.clnt not null; 
key so_id   : abap.char(10) not null; 
item_no     : abap.char(10); 
material_id : abap.char(10); 
@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure : ' zdb_sales_item.uom' 
quantity    : abap.quan(5,2); 
uom         : meins; 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode : ' zdb_sales_item.currency' 
price       : abap.curr(10,2); 
currency    : abap.cuky; 
plant       : werks_d; 
}

2. Created draft tables for both Sales (Header) and Sales (Item) entities to support draft functionality. These tables store intermediate data, allowing users to save incomplete records and activate them later.

@EndUserText.label : 'Draft table for entity ZI_HDR_SALES'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #EXTENSIBLE_ANY
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zdb_header_draft {

  key mandt     : mandt not null;
  key soid      : abap.char(10) not null;
  key draftuuid : sdraft_uuid not null;
  customerid    : abap.char(10);
  orderdate     : sydatum;
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'zdb_header_draft.currency'
  totalamount   : abap.curr(10,2);
  currency      : abap.cuky;
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'zdb_header_draft.currency'
  discountpct   : abap.curr(10,2);
  status        : abap.char(10);
  createdby     : syuname;
  createdon     : timestampl;
  approvedby    : syuname;
  approvedon    : sydatum;
  "%admin"      : include sych_bdl_draft_admin_inc;
}

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Draft table for entity ZI_ITEM_SALES'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #EXTENSIBLE_ANY
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zdb_item_draft {

  key mandt       : mandt not null;
  key soid        : abap.char(10) not null;
  key draftuuid   : sdraft_uuid not null;
  parentdraftuuid : sdraft_uuid;
  itemno          : abap.char(10);
  materialid      : abap.char(10);
  @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure : 'zdb_item_draft.uom'
  quantity        : abap.quan(5,2);
  uom             : meins;
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'zdb_item_draft.currency'
  price           : abap.curr(10,2);
  currency        : abap.cuky;
  plant           : werks_d;
  "%admin"        : include sych_bdl_draft_admin_inc;
}

3. Created an Interface view on top of these two database table. Defined a proper association between Sales (Header) and Sales (Item) using so_id to establish the relationship. 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK 
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface view for sales header table' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 

define root view entity ZI_HDR_SALES  
as select from zdb_sales_hdr 
composition [1..*] of ZI_ITEM_SALES as _salesitem 
{ 
key so_id    as SoId, 
customer_id  as CustomerId, 
order_date   as OrderDate, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'Currency' 
total_amount as TotalAmount, 
currency     as Currency, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'Currency' 
discount_pct as DiscountPct, 
status       as Status, 
created_by   as CreatedBy, 
created_on   as CreatedOn, 
approved_by  as ApprovedBy, 
approved_on  as ApprovedOn, 
_salesitem 
}

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE] 
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface view for sales item table' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@ObjectModel.usageType:{ 
serviceQuality: #X, 
sizeCategory: #S, 
dataClass: #MIXED 
} 
define view entity ZI_ITEM_SALES  
as select from zdb_sales_item 
association to parent ZI_HDR_SALES as _header 
on $projection.SoId = _header.SoId 
{ 
key so_id   as SoId, 
item_no     as ItemNo, 
material_id as MaterialId, 
@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'Uom' 
quantity    as Quantity, 
uom         as Uom, 
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'Currency' 
price       as Price, 
currency    as Currency, 
plant       as Plant, 
_header 
}

4. Created a consumption CDS view on top of the interface views to expose the data for UI usage. Instead of using metadata extensions, all required UI annotations were directly maintained within the consumption view for simplicity. 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK 
@EndUserText.label: 'Projection view for sales header table' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true 

define root view entity ZC_HDR_SALES  
provider contract transactional_query 
as projection on ZI_HDR_SALES 
{ 
@UI.facet: [ 
{ position: 10, label: 'Sales Header', purpose: #STANDARD, type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE }, 
{ position: 20, label: 'Sales Item', purpose: #STANDARD, type: #LINEITEM_REFERENCE, targetElement: '_salesitem' } 
] 

@UI: { 
lineItem: [{ position: 10, label: 'Sales Id' }], 
identification: [{ position: 10, label: 'Sales Id' }] 
} 
SoId; 

@UI: { 
lineItem: [{ position: 20, label: 'Customer Id' }], 
identification: [{ position: 20, label: 'Customer Id' }] 
} 
CustomerId; 

@UI: { 
lineItem: [{ position: 30, label: 'Order date' }], 
identification: [{ position: 30, label: 'Order date' }] 
} 
OrderDate; 

@UI: { 
lineItem: [{ position: 40, label: 'Total amount' }], 
identification: [{ position: 40, label: 'Total Amount' }] 
} 
TotalAmount; 

@UI: { 
lineItem: [{ position: 50, label: 'Currency' }], 
identification: [{ position: 50, label: 'Currency' }] 
} 
Currency; 

@UI: { 
lineItem: [{ position: 60 }], 
identification: [{ position: 60, label: 'Discount price' }] 
} 

@UI.hidden: true 
DiscountPct; 

@UI: { 
lineItem: [{ position: 70, label: 'Order Status' }], 
identification: [{ position: 70, label: 'Status' }] 
} 

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{  
entity:{  
name : 'ZI_NK_STATUS_VH', 
element: 'Status' 
} 
}] 
Status; 

@UI: { 
lineItem: [{ position: 80, label: 'Created by' }], 
identification: [{ position: 80, label: 'Created by' }] 
} 
CreatedBy; 

@UI: { 
lineItem: [{ position: 90, label: 'Created on' }], 
identification: [{ position: 90, label: 'Created on' }] 
} 
CreatedOn; 

@UI: { 
lineItem: [{ position: 100 }], 
identification: [{ position: 100, label: 'Approved by' }] 
} 

@UI.hidden: true 
ApprovedBy; 

@UI: { 
lineItem: [{ position: 110 }], 
identification: [{ position: 110, label: 'Approved on' }] 
} 

@UI.hidden: true 
ApprovedOn; 
_salesitem : redirected to composition child ZC_ITEM_SALES 
}

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED 
@EndUserText.label: 'Projection view for sales itemtable' 
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true 
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true 

define view entity ZC_ITEM_SALES  
as projection on ZI_ITEM_SALES 
{ 
@UI.facet: [{ position: 10, label: 'Sales Item', purpose: #STANDARD, type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE }] 

@UI:{ 
lineItem: [{ position: 10, label: 'Sales Id' }], 
identification: [{ position: 10, label: 'Sales Id' }] 
} 
SoId; 

@UI:{ 
lineItem: [{ position: 20, label: 'Item no'}], 
identification: [{ position: 20, label: 'Item no' }] 
} 
ItemNo; 

@UI:{ 
lineItem: [{ position: 30, label: 'Material Id' }], 
identification: [{ position: 30, label: 'Material id' }] 
} 
MaterialId; 

@UI:{ 
lineItem: [{ position: 40, label: 'Quantity' }], 
identification: [{ position: 40, label: 'Quantity' }] 
} 
Quantity; 

@UI:{ 
lineItem: [{ position: 50, label: 'Unit' }], 
identification: [{ position: 50, label: 'Unit of measure' }] 
} 

@UI.hidden: true 
Uom; 

@UI:{ 
lineItem: [{ position: 60, label: 'Price'}], 
identification: [{ position: 60, label: 'Price' }] 
} 
Price; 

@UI:{ 
lineItem: [{ position: 70 }], 
identification: [{ position: 70, label: 'Currency' }] 
} 
Currency; 

@UI:{ 
lineItem: [{ position: 80, label: 'Plant'}], 
identification: [{ position: 80, label: 'Plant' }] 
} 
Plant; 
_header : redirected to parent ZC_HDR_SALES 
}

5. Created behavior definitions on top of the Sales (Header) interface view. Defined the required operations and enabled features like draft handling, associations, specified lock master (for the root entity) and lock dependent (for child entity) to tell the framework that locking is required as per our requirements. 

Specify lock master (for the root entity). 

define behavior for ZI_HDR_SALES //alias <alias_name> 
draft table zdb_header_draft 
late numbering 
lock master 
total etag CreatedBy 
authorization master ( instance ) 
{ 
create; 
update ( features : instance ); 
delete ( features : instance ); 
field ( readonly ) SoId; 
}

Specify lock dependent (for child entity). 

define behavior for ZI_ITEM_SALES //alias <alias_name> 
draft table zdb_item_draft 
late numbering 
lock dependent by _header 
authorization dependent by _header 
{ 
update; 
delete; 
field ( readonly ) SoId; 
association _header { with draft; } 
}

5. Created a lock object by providing Sales (Header) as Primary table. 

Selected lock mode as write lock. 

Activated, Generated Enqueue and Dequeue Objects. 

6. Implemented the required business logic in the behavior pool class using a helper class. This includes handling calculations, and manual data persistence for both Sales entities. 

Inside the FOR LOCK method in the behavior pool class, must explicitly call the created lock object( ‘EZLOCK_SALES' ) to set the actual database lock. 

METHOD lock. 
TRY. 
DATA(lock) = cl_abap_lock_object_factory=>get_instance( iv_name = 'EZLOCK_SALES' ). 
CATCH cx_abap_lock_failure INTO DATA(exception). 
RAISE SHORTDUMP exception. 
ENDTRY. 

LOOP AT keys ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_data>). 
TRY. 
lock->enqueue( 
it_parameter = VALUE #( ( name = 'Sales Id' value = REF #( <fs_data>-SoId ) ) ) 
). 
CATCH cx_abap_foreign_lock INTO DATA(foreign_lock). 

APPEND VALUE #( 
SoId = keys[ 1 ]-SoId 
%msg = new_message_with_text( 
severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error 
text = 'Record is locked by ' && foreign_lock->user_name 
) 
) TO reported-zi_hdr_sales. 

APPEND VALUE #( 
SoId = keys[ 1 ]-SoId 
) TO failed-zi_hdr_sales.
